revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Eta Funayama Kofun]][[ja:江田船山古墳]][[ko:에타후나야마 고분]][[zh:江田船山古墳]] {{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{more citations needed|date=July 2021}}
[[File:Etafunayama2008010201.jpg|thumb|Eta Funayama Kofun]] {{coord|32|58|16.63|N|130|36|0.02|E|region:JP-43_scale:20000|display=title}}
'''Eta Funayama Kofun''' ({{Langx|ja|江田船山古墳}}) is a ''[[kofun]]'', or {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|qid=Q34023|lt=burial mound|1=Tumulus}}, located in {{ill|Nagomi, Kumamoto|en|Nagomi, Kumamoto|qid=Q1156415|lt=Nagomi, Kumamoto|1=Nagomi, Kumamoto}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=江田船山古墳 / 観光情報TOP / 和水町|url=https://www.town.nagomi.lg.jp/kankou/hpkiji/pub/detail.aspx?c_id=3&id=707&class_id=766|access-date=2021-07-08|website=www.town.nagomi.lg.jp}}</ref> The mound was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref>{{Cite web|last=第２版|first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),デジタル大辞泉,国指定史跡ガイド,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,百科事典マイペディア,防府市歴史用語集,精選版 日本国語大辞典,旺文社日本史事典 三訂版,世界大百科事典|title=江田船山古墳とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B1%9F%E7%94%B0%E8%88%B9%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-36675|access-date=2021-07-08|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> The designation includes {{nihongo|Tsukabōzu Kofun|塚坊主古墳}} and {{nihongo|Kokuzōtsuka Kofun|虚空蔵塚古墳}}. Several artifacts excavated from the mound have been designated {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} are now at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} (see {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)|qid=Q6591583|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)}}).<ref>{{cite web|script-title=ja:肥後江田船山古墳出土品|trans-title=Excavated Artefacts from Higo Eda Funayama Kofun|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/864|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|language=Japanese|accessdate=28 June 2012}}</ref> The style of the bronze items resemble artifacts from the Korean kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], which had many exchanges with Japan at the time.<ref>[https://colbase.nich.go.jp/collection_items/tnm/J-786?locale=en Gilt Bronze Crown, Tokyo National Museum]</ref>
== Eta Funayama Sword == [[File:江田船山古墳出土 銀象嵌銘大刀.JPG|thumb|The Eta Funayama Sword, made of iron with inlaid silver inscription]] The {{Nihongo|Eta Funayama Burial Mound Sword|江田船山古墳鉄剣|eta funayama kofun tekken}} is a 5th-century ancient iron sword excavated from the mound in 1873. The inscription on the blade indicates that the sword was made during the era of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} in the 5th century. This sword, along with other items from the mound, have been designated {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in the {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)|qid=Q6591583|lt=category archaeological materials|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)}}.
=== Inscription === The original inscription and translation is as follows:<ref name="Seeley">Seeley, Christopher. ''A History of Writing in Japan''. [[:en:Brill Academic Publishers|Brill Academic Publishers]]. 1991. pp 24–25. {{ISBN|9004090819}}.</ref>{{blockquote|治天下獲□□□鹵大王，奉□典曹人名无□弖，八月中，用大錡釜併四尺廷刀八十練□十捃□寸上□□刀。服此刀者□□子孫注ゝ得其恩也，不失其所統。作刀者名伊太加，書者張安也。}}{{blockquote|In the reign of the great ruler Wa[kataki]ru who ruled the land, in the eighth month an official in service by the name of Mu...te used a large cauldron and a four-foot court [?] sword. Refining and selecting the metal many times,... sword. He who wears this sword...; his descendants will successively obtain its favours, and not lose that which they control. The name of the person who made the sword is Itaka; the text was written by Chōan.}}
Portions of the text are now illegible (rendered above as □ in the Japanese inscription, or {{ill|Ellipsis|en|Ellipsis|qid=Q32518|lt=ellipses|1=Ellipsis}} in the English translation), making it difficult to interpret. The name of the ruler, Wakatakiru, is reconstructed from evidence on the {{ill|Inariyama Sword|en|Inariyama Sword|qid=Q856395|lt=Inariyama burial mound sword|1=Inariyama Sword}}.
==Excavated items== <gallery mode=packed widths="240px" heights="240px"> Periodo kofun, armatura dal tumulo Eta-Funayama, V-VI sec.JPG|Iron armor set with ''[[tankō]]'' style cuirass Gilt bronze crown from the Eta Funayama Tomb - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06418.JPG|Bronze crown with traces of gilding Mirror with Design of Divinities and Animals, China, from the Eta Funayama Tomb - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06414.JPG|Bronze mirror of Chinese origin Slippers from Eta Funayama Tumulus, Nagomi-machi, Kumamoto, Kofun period, 5th-6th century, gilt bronze - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05636.JPG|Bronze shoes with hexagonal pattern </gallery>
== Note == {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{cite book | last = Seeley | first = Christopher | title = A History of Writing in Japan | publisher = [[:en:Brill Academic Publishers|Brill Academic Publishers]] | year = 1991 | isbn = 9004090819 | pages = 24–25 | url = https://books.google.com/books?id=KCZ2ya6cg88C&pg=PA24 }}
[[en:Eta Funayama Kofun]]
{{wikidata link|Q11259227}} [[Category:Individual Japanese swords]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
